construct: Applies scientific skills for sustainable food production and entrepreneurship.

elementsOfConstruct.description: Investigates agricultural scientific problem through observations, measurement, experimentation and recording.

subSkills.label: Identifies and defines agricultural problems and plans appropriate investigations
subSkills.note: Skill-lens EoC, not a topic bucket. Exemplar was built on Climate-Smart Agriculture content but the EoC label describes investigation methodology, not a subject area. Provisional routing only.

elementsOfConstruct.description: Demonstrates practical skills in managing crops for improved yields.

subSkills.label: Applies practical skills in crop management to solve real life problems and improve productivity
topicAliases: External and Internal Morphology of Plants

elementsOfConstruct.description: Applies practical skills in managing livestock for improved production.

subSkills.label: Applies practical skills in Livestock management to solve real life problems and improve productivity

elementsOfConstruct.description: Analyses data and applies sustainable scientific techniques for improved productivity.

subSkills.label: Analyses and interprets agricultural data to draw valid scientific conclusions; applies sustainable agricultural techniques
subSkills.note: Skill-lens EoC. Exemplar blended Farm Mechanisation AND Crop Production content in one scenario. Soil Science and Farm Structures/Machinery routed here provisionally as closest content fit -- not exemplar-confirmed for Soil Science specifically.

elementsOfConstruct.description: Applies agribusiness and entrepreneurship skills in managing farm enterprises.

subSkills.label: Develops and manages profitable agricultural enterprises through business planning, financial management, marketing, risk management, value addition and record keeping

elementsOfConstruct.description: Investigates agricultural problems and demonstrates practical skills in real life situations.

subSkills.label: Identifies agricultural problems and applies practical skills to solve them
unverifiedTopicAliases: Factors that Influence Agricultural Development in Zambia
Land Tenure Systems and Land Use
subSkills.note: SBA-only EoC (not in the 5-EoC Final Exam spec, no exemplar provided). Provisionally anchored to the foundational Agriculture in Zambia topic -- unconfirmed, no exemplar evidence either way.

components.structureNotes: 12 tasks total across Form 2+3 (6 each), all 6 EoC, includes a Field Project (non-comparative, Form 2) worth 20 marks.
12 tasks total across Form 2+3 (6 each), all 6 EoC, includes a Comparative Research (Form 3) worth 20 marks.
5 compulsory items, one per EoC1-5 (EoC6 excluded from Final Exam). Each item = 1 scenario + 2 tasks. Marks: EoC1=15, EoC2=10, EoC3=10, EoC4=15, EoC5=10. Duration 2 hours.

rules: SBA: teachers develop scoring guides/rubrics per task; measures correctness plus scientific skills (observation, data interpretation, problem solving, communication, procedure use).
Final Exam: ECZ-developed scoring guides per EoC; examiner coordination meeting held before marking to agree acceptable alternative answers.
Marks available per item shown in brackets [ ] at the end of the item.
Number of marks per item depends on difficulty -- number of cognitive processes and amount of writing required.
Where a response needs units, failure to supply the correct unit forfeits the mark even if the numerical value is correct.
Where working is required and a candidate supplies a correct numerical answer with wrong or no working shown, no marks are awarded.

exemplars.scenarioTheme: Rural district farmers using traditional burning/clearing/overgrazing practices causing climate change: explain causes, evaluate national agricultural impact
Farm manager using agricultural drones to fix a wheat farm's planting, fertiliser, irrigation, monitoring and harvesting problems
Mixed poultry farmer feeding broilers and layers the same feed causing slow broiler growth/leg weakness and thin layer eggshells: explain cause, formulate corrected feed, suggest corrective actions

Commercial vegetable farmer relying on manual labour for land prep/irrigation/fertiliser facing declining cabbage/tomato yield and quality: analyse causes, explain other factors, recommend mechanisation
Maize/soya farmer facing diminishing fertiliser returns and limited capital: apply law of diminishing returns, apply opportunity cost, suggest management strategies
